title: Executive Order 12041—Generalized System of Preferences for Developing Countries

flags.justification: Exercises specific statutory modification authority (Trade Act §504(c)); bounded and narrow.
Precise technical amendments with specific annex and tariff-item cross-references.
Multiple technical provisions all implement one statute, Title V of the Trade Act of 1974.
Modifies existing eligibility criteria that are themselves objective and numeric (per EO 11888).
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
Standing-procedural-framework category applies: this amends EO 11888, itself a periodically-revised framework.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
Eligibility remains governed by objective, auditable criteria.
A routine technical amendment; no novel authority claim.
No conflicting agency mandate is implicated.
Amends the existing 'A*' competitive-need exclusion annexes, narrow and specifically enumerated, consistent with EO 11888.
